title: Aurora Cannabis acquires Safari Flower in $26.5 million cash and stock deal
text: StratCann reports Aurora Cannabis acquires Safari Flower Company in strategic capacity expansion move. Deal overview: $26.5 million acquisition combining cash and stock consideration. Purchase expands EU GMP-certified cultivation capacity for international medical markets. Strategic rationale: Aurora adding 59,000 sq ft EU GMP certified facility. Acquisition enhances ability to serve Germany, UK, Poland, and Australia markets. Timing aligns with demand surge for EU GMP medical cannabis products. Financial terms: Cash component $15 million with additional $2 million contingent payment. Stock issuance component provides equity upside to Safari Flower shareholders. Enterprise value $26.5 million reflects acquisition price and assumed debt. Expected EBITDA impact: Positive adjusted EBITDA contribution expected fiscal 2027. Further benefits anticipated in 2028 as facility reaches full utilization. Manufacturing capacity: 59,000 sq ft facility significantly expands Aurora's production footprint. Adds certified capacity for GMP-required product manufacturing. Complements existing Canadian and Australian operations. Market timing: EU medical cannabis demand growing substantially post-legalization in major markets. Germany's descheduling creating significant patient growth opportunities. Aurora positioning to capture market share in expanded patient base. Strategic fit: Acquisition extends Aurora's integrated supply chain across key markets. Vertical integration enables margin optimization. Manufacturing diversity reduces supply risk and enhances reliability.
